jokes.text: I used a guy this weekend. Yeah, Gwen locked me out. By accident.
jokes.setup: Roland casually mentions using a locksmith
jokes.punchline: The 'by accident' suggests marital tension with his wife locking him out intentionally
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment revealing marital dynamics through understated delivery

jokes.text: Your wife's pregnant in her 40's. How many surprises do you need?
jokes.setup: Roland wants to keep the baby's gender a surprise
jokes.punchline: Ronnie points out the pregnancy itself is already surprising enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ronnie character moment - blunt, practical, cutting through sentimental nonsense with brutal honesty

jokes.text: It's either my birthday, or Gloria Estefan's.
jokes.setup: Roland needs to guess the safe combination
jokes.punchline: His only two guesses are his birthday or Gloria Estefan's - oddly specific and random
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Roland moment - the specificity of Gloria Estefan makes it memorable and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Mucho Dias, mes amis.
jokes.setup: Character entering with greeting
jokes.punchline: Mixes Spanish and French incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism showing character pretension. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Okay, turns out it's Gloria's, September 1, 57.
jokes.setup: Earlier guess about Gloria Estefan's birthday
jokes.punchline: It actually was Gloria Estefan's birthday, validating the absurd guess
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that pays off the earlier absurd setup perfectly

jokes.text: Who put a picture of a ghost on my desk?
jokes.setup: Ronnie finds an unfamiliar image on her desk
jokes.punchline: She mistakes a sonogram for a ghost picture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ronnie's practical, no-nonsense perspective on medical imagery

jokes.text: Roland, why should I be the only one encumbered with this emotional cargo?
jokes.setup: Ronnie accidentally learned the baby's gender
jokes.punchline: She describes knowing a baby's gender as 'emotional cargo' - overly dramatic language for simple information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ronnie character work - her overly formal language for mundane situations

jokes.text: might you and I confabulate for a moment in the back room?
jokes.setup: Moira wants to talk privately with Ronnie
jokes.punchline: Uses 'confabulate' (an overly fancy word for 'chat') in typical Moira fashion
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary showcase - perfectly in character but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: No, Moira, I'm not falling for that one.
jokes.setup: Moira uses fancy language to request a private conversation
jokes.punchline: Ronnie immediately sees through Moira's verbal manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Ronnie's immunity to Moira's verbal theatrics

jokes.text: I assure you it's a salacious bit of Council-related news.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to entice Ronnie into private conversation
jokes.punchline: 'Salacious' and 'Council-related' are contradictory - town council business is the opposite of salacious
jokes.explanation: Solid Moira moment using inappropriate vocabulary to make mundane things sound exciting

jokes.text: Maybe the storm warning was for yesterday.
jokes.setup: David asks how to explain yesterday's lack of customers if today's storm explains today's lack
jokes.punchline: Stevie's absurd logic that the storm warning might have been retroactive
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie deadpan moment with illogical but delivered-with-confidence reasoning

jokes.text: Okay, I don't have all the answers.
jokes.setup: David continues pressing her about the lack of customers
jokes.punchline: Stevie gives up immediately rather than continue trying to explain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deflection - admits defeat rather than continue the charade

jokes.text: Or, hey, just like a way. Just one way to engage with the community.
jokes.setup: David suggests finding better ways to engage with the community
jokes.punchline: Immediately lowers his expectations to just finding ANY way to engage
jokes.explanation: Good David moment showing his tendency to immediately lower expectations when faced with reality

jokes.text: Yes! It was opening day, and there was booze. And people are drunks.
jokes.setup: Patrick reminds David that people showed up on opening day
jokes.punchline: David cynically attributes the attendance to free alcohol, not their business
jokes.explanation: Classic David pessimism - deflating any positive interpretation with cynical reality

jokes.text: I am open to suggestions.
jokes.setup: Patrick suggests hosting an open mic night
jokes.punchline: David's flat rejection disguised as openness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective David moment - saying the opposite of what he means

jokes.text: Oh god. The occasional improve troupe would stop by. I'm um... I'm feeling kind of ill.
jokes.setup: Patrick describes his high school open mic experience including improv
jokes.punchline: David has a physical reaction to the mention of improv troupes
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his horror at amateur theater is so intense it causes physical symptoms

jokes.text: Okay, no, worst case scenario, I watch improv.
jokes.setup: Patrick says worst case they get people in the store
jokes.punchline: David corrects him - watching improv is worse than business failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent David line - perfect character work showing his priorities and delivery is economical and quotable

jokes.text: They're just a placebo effect for people who feel the need to complain.
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions comment cards
jokes.punchline: Stevie reveals she doesn't actually read customer feedback
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie character moment - her casual dismissal of customer service basics

jokes.text: Those cards are written by embittered people with way too much time on their hands. A lot of these are from Mom.
jokes.setup: Stevie dismisses comment cards as coming from bitter people
jokes.punchline: Reveals many are from her own mother
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up generic complaint then reveals personal family dynamic

jokes.text: it's just like a cartoon of a construction worker holding a sign that says 'we'll be right back'.
jokes.setup: Stevie and Johnny claim they have a website
jokes.punchline: Alexis describes the generic 'under construction' placeholder page
jokes.explanation: Solid joke about outdated web practices, relatable but not exceptional

jokes.text: I had strep at the time.
jokes.setup: Alexis criticizes the phone booking woman's aggressive voice
jokes.punchline: Stevie casually reveals she WAS the aggressive phone voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie delivery - matter-of-fact admission that explains the problem while showing she hasn't fixed it

jokes.text: the woman's voice was aggressive and off-putting. / I had strep at the time.
jokes.setup: Alexis criticizes phone booking system voice
jokes.punchline: Stevie reveals it was her with strep throat
jokes.explanation: Good reveal joke. Stevie's deadpan delivery of medical excuse is perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Um, I actually prefer to be called a freelance brand invigorator.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if they'd call Alexis a publicist
jokes.punchline: Alexis prefers an unnecessarily fancy and made-up job title
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis character work - inventing impressive-sounding but meaningless job titles

jokes.text: That's great, because Step 1 of the plan was this presentation, and we nailed it.
jokes.setup: Johnny wants to explore Step 1 of Alexis's plan
jokes.punchline: The presentation itself was Step 1 - there was no actual plan yet
jokes.explanation: Great Alexis moment showing her ability to BS her way through situations with confidence

jokes.text: his great-great-great great-grandmother accurately guessed the sex of her daughter's baby, and then was put on trial for witchcraft
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explains the family tradition of keeping gender secret
jokes.punchline: The origin story is absurdly dramatic - witchcraft trial for guessing a baby's gender
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd backstory that escalates from simple tradition to Salem witch trials

jokes.text: Toil and trouble.
jokes.setup: Discussion of witchcraft trials
jokes.punchline: Moira quotes Macbeth's witches, showing off her theater knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character moment - can't resist a theatrical reference

jokes.text: Is it a girl? Valiant attempt, Jocelyn.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn tries to trick Moira into revealing the gender
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately sees through the obvious ploy
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Moira's condescending praise for Jocelyn's failed attempt

jokes.text: Stupid witch!
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion of witchcraft in the family history
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn blames the historical witch for current predicament
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that connects the historical story to current frustration

jokes.text: This is called an acoustic guitar.
jokes.setup: David asks what Patrick is holding
jokes.punchline: Patrick condescendingly explains what an acoustic guitar is
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Patrick deadpan - treating David's horror as ignorance

jokes.text: I mean, if you are, confident, to put yourself and our relationship at risk like that, then I am... 87% behind you.
jokes.setup: David expresses concern about Patrick performing
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific percentage and framing it as relationship risk
jokes.explanation: Perfect David anxiety - the specific percentage and dramatic framing of acoustic guitar as relationship threat

jokes.text: Or more importantly, do I wear anything under it?
jokes.setup: Patrick asks about wearing his fringed vest
jokes.punchline: Escalates to potentially going shirtless under the vest
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke showing Patrick's obliviousness to how cringey he's being

jokes.text: What the fuck does that mean?
jokes.setup: Alexis tells Johnny to 'smile with your eyes'
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt response to pretentious photography direction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment cutting through Alexis's photography BS with direct confusion

jokes.text: Okay now, three more steps to your left.
jokes.setup: Alexis asks Johnny to take one step left
jokes.punchline: She keeps moving him further out of frame
jokes.explanation: Good visual escalation joke - clear progression of removing Johnny from the photo

jokes.text: Pretty country girl who doesn't care about big city things like... dressing well, or how she looks.
jokes.setup: Alexis explains her marketing angle for Stevie
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment describing Stevie's lack of care about appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis character moment - well-meaning but insulting marketing concept

jokes.text: Stevie's Place sounds like a shelter for battered men.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests naming the motel 'Stevie's Place'
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dark interpretation of what that name implies
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stevie line - unexpected dark turn that's both character-appropriate and genuinely funny

jokes.text: call it the Budd Inn. And now that I'm saying that out loud, obviously we're not gonna do that.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests using Stevie's last name for the motel
jokes.punchline: Realizes the sexual innuendo immediately after saying it
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Alexis catches her own innuendo mistake

jokes.text: Don't take it personally, Mr. Rose, it's probably because you didn't know what to do with your hands. Or maybe it's because your face looked like it tasted something sour.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks why they're focusing on solo shots
jokes.punchline: Ray lists increasingly specific criticisms of Johnny's photo performance
jokes.explanation: Good Ray character moment - his blunt honesty delivered as helpful feedback

jokes.text: All of a sudden, expected attention has me doubting what I saw. I barely glanced at that paper.
jokes.setup: Everyone wants to know the baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Moira admits she barely looked and now doubts herself under pressure
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment showing how pressure affects her dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: You told me to keep it a secret, and that's exactly what I did, even from myself.
jokes.setup: Moira is blamed for forgetting what she saw
jokes.punchline: She claims she kept the secret so well she hid it from herself
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira logic - taking secret-keeping to an absurd extreme that somehow makes sense for her character

jokes.text: Well, the sticky note, I believe, is also in the safe.
jokes.setup: Roland wrote the new combination on a sticky note
jokes.punchline: He put the combination note inside the locked safe
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the safe situation - Roland's solution creates a bigger problem

jokes.text: Well that, I will admit, is my blunder.
jokes.setup: Roland realizes he locked the combination inside the safe
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling it just a 'blunder'
jokes.explanation: Good Roland character moment - his casual admission of a major mistake

jokes.text: You're probably thinking like, oh, is my foundation off by a shade? Am I wearing the wrong top? And the bottom line is, maybe.
jokes.setup: Alexis tries to reassure nervous Stevie about the photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Her 'reassurance' confirms Stevie's appearance might actually be wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - trying to be supportive but making it worse with her honesty

jokes.text: the more likely you are to take off, and then I will be left here stuck with way more responsibility than I ever asked for.
jokes.setup: Discussion about growing the business
jokes.punchline: Stevie's fear that success will lead to abandonment and responsibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stevie character work - her anxiety about success leading to more work and abandonment

jokes.text: Johnny's looking half-normal.
jokes.setup: Alexis shows Ray the group photo
jokes.punchline: Damning with faint praise - 'half-normal' is apparently an improvement
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis backhanded compliment - treating 'half-normal' as a victory

jokes.text: Okay, this was not my idea.
jokes.setup: Moira comments on David allowing Patrick to perform
jokes.punchline: David's panicked denial of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Standard David panic response - immediately distancing himself from potential embarrassment

jokes.text: You'll let me know if I should pull the fire alarm?
jokes.setup: David and Moira watch Patrick prepare to sing
jokes.punchline: Moira offers to create an emergency to stop the performance
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how far Moira will go to help David avoid embarrassment

jokes.text: A boy, I believe I told you that.
jokes.setup: Roland and Jocelyn announce it's a boy
jokes.punchline: Moira takes credit despite earlier admitting she wasn't sure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character work - revising history to take credit despite earlier uncertainty

jokes.text: Why isn't it just Rose Motel? The Rosebud makes it a sad stunted thing.
jokes.setup: Looking at the new motel sign reading 'The Rosebud Motel'
jokes.punchline: David's critique of the name as sounding 'stunted'
jokes.explanation: Good David observation about naming - his aesthetic sense applied to business branding

jokes.text: Okay, well that would just look insane.
jokes.setup: Learning Budd has two d's
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately rejects the correct spelling as looking wrong
jokes.explanation: Standard Alexis moment prioritizing appearance over accuracy

jokes.text: not to brag, but this re-brand got me 77% on my final marketing exam.
jokes.setup: Alexis proud of her motel rebranding work
jokes.punchline: Brags about mediocre grade while claiming not to brag
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis obliviousness. Bragging about 77% while saying 'not to brag' shows her skewed perspective on success.

jokes.text: Enough, David.
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions getting 77% on her exam and David presumably makes a comment about her grades
jokes.punchline: Johnny's weary shut-down of David's criticism
jokes.explanation: Simple family dynamic moment - Johnny shutting down sibling bickering
